meeting.purpose_one_line: Discovery call — Priya asked for a 1-pager on our eval-layer approach after her LinkedIn post on Claude eval-debt; she is open to deeper conversation on agentic systems for UK utilities CX

context_summary: Engaged account, 3 touches in 5 days. Priya posted publicly about eval-debt 5 days ago and asked for our approach — she is technically deep and likely the hiring manager for an open Marketing Engineering Lead role at ExampleBPM. The UK utilities expansion from May is the underlying business driver.

recent_signals_relevant.signal: LinkedIn post on Claude eval-debt accruing silently for 6 weeks then surfacing all at once
recent_signals_relevant.implication_for_meeting: This is the explicit pain — open the call by referencing it specifically, not generically

recent_signals_relevant.signal: Open req for Marketing Engineering Lead, Agentic Systems
recent_signals_relevant.implication_for_meeting: She is hiring for this — frame ourselves as partner-to-this-hire, not replacement

recent_signals_relevant.signal: UK utilities BPM contract expansion announced
recent_signals_relevant.implication_for_meeting: The agentic systems work is downstream of this commercial commitment — there is real budget and timeline pressure

talking_points: Open with: 'Your post about eval-debt surfacing at week 6 — that is exactly the failure mode our regression-gate prevents, want to walk through how we structure the golden set?'
When she mentions the UK utilities expansion, name the three signal types (outage, billing, digital transformation) — shows you have read the public commitment and thought about the data fabric
If the Marketing Engineering Lead role comes up, position as partner-of-choice — we typically work alongside that hire on the eval and integration layer, not in place of them

one_risk_or_objection.risk: Priya may want to evaluate Claude-vs-in-house build, with us as the in-house enabler — risk is she sees us as a vendor she will replace once she hires the Lead
one_risk_or_objection.how_to_handle: Pre-empt by naming it: 'Most CTO conversations I have go to build-vs-buy in the first 10 minutes — happy to share where we add leverage vs where you should own it in-house from day one.' That earns her trust and surfaces the decision instead of letting it sit underneath.

one_question_to_ask: What does your current eval setup actually catch today — is it pre-deployment regression, production output monitoring, or both? And where does it fall short?
do_not_say: Do not pitch generic 'AI for marketing' — she has shipped Claude in production and will dismiss vague AI talk
Do not reference HubSpot or Salesforce as if we have built integrations there — we have not; pivot to data-fabric concepts if needed
Do not mention the Marketing Engineering Lead hire unless she brings it up — she may not know we saw the JD and it could read as creepy
